verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I TM protein but resides predominantly in the ER membrane, where its luminal EF-hand senses Ca2+ store depletion and it clusters at ER-PM junctions to gate Orai channels. A documented minor pool reaches the plasma membrane (surface-exposed N-terminus detected by surface biotinylation/antibodies in multiple cell types), giving it a real but minority outer-leaflet presence alongside its dominant ER home. This fits dual_localization rather than a classical surface receptor call.
